claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To give you an accurate assessment, I need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you responsible for, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**First, how old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 19 years old, I need to understand their current situation to determine eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education - doing A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. A-levels at sixth form college counts as approved education, which is good.

Now I need to understand your living situation:

**Do you live with Alex, or does Alex live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. Now I need to check a few more important details about your circumstances.

**Are you currently working, and if so, what is your approximate annual income?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do work, yes. Is my income relevant for whether I can claim Child Benefit?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's a good question! Your income can be relevant in a specific way. While your income doesn't affect whether you *qualify* for Child Benefit, if your individual income is over £60,000 per year, you may have to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit you receive through a tax charge called the "High Income Child Benefit tax charge."

So for now, let me ask:

**What is your approximate annual income?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My income is below £60,000, so that shouldn't be an issue.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that. 

Before I can give you my final assessment, I need to check one more important detail:

**Are you a UK resident, and do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me review your circumstances:
- You have one child: Alex
- Alex is 19 years old and currently in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college)
- Alex lives with you at home
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Your income is below £60,000

Based on the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. However, for children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they're under 20 and staying in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 19 and in approved education (A-levels), you meet the eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the necessary criteria:
- Alex is 19 years old and under 20
- Alex is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college)
- You live with Alex in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex

Since your income is also below £60,000, you won't face the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

You can now go ahead and apply for Child Benefit if you haven't already done so. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion. Additionally, you live with Alex in the UK and are responsible for them. All eligibility requirements are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is 19 years old, in approved education (A-levels), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. The key eligibility criterion for children aged 16 or over is that they must be under 20 and in approved education or training, which Alex satisfies. Your income is also below the £60,000 threshold where the High Income Child Benefit tax charge would apply.">

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion. Additionally, you live with Alex in the UK and are responsible for them. All eligibility requirements are met.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is 19 years old, in approved education (A-levels), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. The key eligibility criterion for children aged 16 or over is that they must be under 20 and in approved education or training, which Alex satisfies. Your income is also below the £60,000 threshold where the High Income Child Benefit tax charge would apply.">
